relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul’s heart and prayer remain directed toward Israel’s saving/healing.
Zeal is real, but needs fuller knowing.
Self-established justice misses the Divine justice being offered.
Messiah is Law’s aim and completion for everyone trusting.
Moses names life through doing the Law.
Trust-justice does not need to climb heaven to fetch Messiah.
Trust-justice does not need to descend into abyss to raise Messiah.
The word is near: mouth, heart, trust.
Mouth-confession and heart-trust open saving/healing.
Heart trusts into justice; mouth confesses into saving/healing.
Everyone trusting upon him will not be shamed.
No distinction remains before the one Lord rich to all who call.
Everyone calling on the Lord’s name is saved/healed.
Calling requires trust; trust requires hearing; hearing requires announcement.
Sent feet become beautiful through good news.
Not all obey the report of good news.
Trust is born through hearing the Messiah-word.
The witness has gone out to earth’s ends.
Moses already spoke of provocation through what was not counted as a nation.
Isaiah boldly says the Divine is found by those not seeking.
All day, the Divine’s hands stretch toward a resisting people.

contemplative_note: Romans 10 keeps prayer, grief, and nearness together. The word is not far away: mouth, heart, trust, and the name become the access point.
